questions.text: A student records himself singing a song for his music project. The recorded sound is converted to binary to be processed by a computer. Give one input device the student can use to record the song.

A student records himself singing a song for his music project. The student uses sound editing software to edit the recorded sound. Tick (✓) one box to show whether sound editing software is an example of application, security, system or utility software.
A application software
B security software
C system software
D utility software

A student records himself singing a song for his music project. Two binary numbers stored from the recording are 00011001 and 10110100. Convert the two binary numbers to denary numbers.
00011001
10110100

A student records himself singing a song for his music project. Two binary numbers stored from the recording are 00011001 and 10110100. Convert the two binary numbers to hexadecimal numbers.
00011001
10110100

A student records himself singing a song for his music project. A logical right shift of two places is performed on the binary number 10100100. Give the binary number that would be stored after the logical shift has taken place.

A student records himself singing a song for his music project. The two's complement 8-bit binary integer 11001001 is also stored. Convert the two's complement 8-bit binary integer to denary. Show all of your working.
Working space
Denary value

answers.scoring_rule: 1 mark for correct method of working, 1 mark for correct answer

marking_points.text: One mark for correct method of working e.g. flip and add 1

questions.text:
A student records himself singing a song for his music project. The table contains terms and descriptions about the process of converting analogue sound to binary. Complete the table with the missing term and descriptions.
| Term | Description |
|---|---|
| [blank] | This is the measurement of the height (amplitude) of a sound wave taken at regular time intervals. |
| sample rate | [blank] |
| sample resolution | [blank] |

visuals.description: Table with terms (sample, sample rate, sample resolution) and their descriptions about converting analogue sound to binary

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct missing term or definition.

marking_points.text: (sound) sampling/sample — This is the measurement of the height (amplitude) of a sound wave at regular time intervals.
sample rate — The number of samples recorded each second
sample resolution — The number of bits that are used to record each sample

questions.text: A company employee is editing a video for the company's website. Data is stored in the random access memory (RAM) when the video is edited. The RAM becomes full and pages of data are transmitted to a partitioned section of the secondary storage to stop the computer from crashing. Give the name of the partitioned section of the secondary storage that is used in this process.

A company employee is editing a video for the company's website. The secondary storage is a hard disk drive (HDD). An HDD is an example of magnetic storage. One feature of magnetic storage is that it uses platters. Give three other features of magnetic storage.
1
2
3

marking_points.text: Platters rotate (to allow data to be read) // has moving parts.

questions.text: A company employee is editing a video for the company's website. The secondary storage is a hard disk drive (HDD). Give the reasons why an HDD is an example of secondary storage.

marking_points.text: It is not directly accessed by the CPU.
It stores data permanently until it is deleted by the user // non-volatile.

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has several hardware components. Complete the table with the missing terms and descriptions about computer hardware.
| Term | Description |
|---|---|
| [blank] | This is the address given to a network interface card (NIC) when it is manufactured. |
| register | [blank] |
| clock | [blank] |
| control unit (CU) | [blank] |
| [blank] | This is a processing unit within the CPU that can fetch, decode and execute instructions. |

visuals.description: Table with hardware terms (MAC address, register, clock, control unit, core) and their descriptions

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct missing term or description.

marking_points.text: MAC address — This is the address given to a network interface card (NIC) when it is manufactured.
register — A component in the central processing unit (CPU) that is used to temporarily store data.
clock — It controls the number of fetch-decode-execute cycles that are performed per second.
control unit — A component in the CPU that manages the flow of data around the CPU // A component that decodes the instructions
core — This is a processing unit within the CPU that can fetch, decode and execute instructions.

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has several hardware components. Identify three registers that are used in the fetch stage of the fetch–decode–execute (FDE) cycle.
1
2
3

A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has several hardware components. Circle the two buses that are used in the fetch stage of the FDE cycle.
binary  control  denary  data
information  hardware  software
A computer has an operating system. One function of the operating system is handling interrupts. Describe the role of the operating system in handling interrupts.

marking_points.text: It assigns a priority to an interrupt.
It uses an interrupt handler / interrupt service routine to process the interrupt.

questions.text:
A computer has an operating system. One function of the operating system is handling interrupts. Give two other functions of an operating system.
1
2

marking_points.text: providing a platform for running applications

questions.text: A computer has an operating system. Hardware and software interrupts are two types of interrupt. Give one example of a hardware interrupt.
A computer has an operating system. Hardware and software interrupts are two types of interrupt. Give one example of a software interrupt.

marking_points.text: two processes trying to access the same memory location

questions.text: An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The user types in the uniform resource locator (URL) for the website and this is sent to a domain name server (DNS). Describe the role of the DNS.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: storing a database of URLs and matching IP addresses
search its database for the matching IP address.
send the URL to another DNS if URL cannot be found.
return the IP address to the web browser

questions.text:
An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The website asks a user to input their username and password to log into their account. The company want to make their login system more secure. Tick (✓) one box to show one other security method that could be added to make the login system more secure.
A access levels
B firewall
C spyware
D two-step verification

An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The company uses a proxy server to keep the web server safe from distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks. Complete and annotate a diagram to show how a DDoS attack is carried out on the web server.
web server

visuals.description: Partially drawn diagram showing a web server, to be completed and annotated to illustrate a DDoS attack

Diagram showing: third party sending malware to computers, computers turned into bots forming a botnet, third party initiating attack, all bots sending requests at once to web server, web server crashing

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each part of the diagram that shows:

marking_points.text: A perpetrator/third party sending malware // user downloads/installs malware
Each computer is turned into a bot …
All the bots send a request at once to the web server.

questions.text:
An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The company uses a proxy server to keep the web server safe from distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks. Identify two tasks that can be performed by a proxy server that are not performed by a firewall.
1
2

An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The company uses cookies to allow users to keep items in a shopping cart. The items remain in the cart after the user closes their web browser. Tick (✓) one box to show the type of cookie that is used for this process.
A dynamic
B persistent
C session
D static
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

questions.text:
An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The company uses cookies to allow users to keep items in a shopping cart. Give three other ways that the company can use cookies.
1
2
3
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

questions.text:
An online company has a website that allows a user to buy products. The company uses cookies to allow users to keep items in a shopping cart. Cookies are stored by a web browser. Give two other functions of a web browser.
1
2
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

questions.text: A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. The automated system uses sensors. Give one other component that would be part of the automated system.

A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. One sensor that is part of the automated system is a temperature sensor. Give one other sensor that could be part of the automated system.

A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. An alert is sent to an employee in the weather office if a temperature above 40 °C is detected. Explain how a temperature above 40 °C is detected for the alert.

marking_points.text: Microprocessor received data from the sensor.
Microprocessor compares data against preset data of 40.
If the data is greater than 40 the microprocessor sends a signal to trigger the alert.

questions.text: A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. An alert is sent to an employee in the weather office if a temperature above 40 °C is detected. The data for the alert is sent using serial simplex data transmission. Explain how the data is sent using serial simplex data transmission.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Data is sent one bit at a time.
Data can only be sent in one direction.

questions.text:
A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. Give two advantages to the weather station employees of using an automated weather system.
1
2

marking_points.text: Employees don't need to carry out a mundane/repetitive task.
Employees don't need to work overnight.
Employees don't need to work outside in adverse weather conditions.

questions.text: A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. Give one disadvantage to the weather station employees of using an automated weather system.

marking_points.text: May replace employees and take their job

questions.text: A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. The automated weather system is upgraded and given machine learning capabilities. Machine learning is part of a branch of computer science. Identify the name of this branch of computer science.

A weather station uses automated systems to collect and analyse data about the weather. The automated weather system is upgraded and given machine learning capabilities. Explain how the machine learning capabilities can be used to predict future weather.

marking_points.text: It can adapt its own processes/data.
It could analyse the data for trends/patterns.
… and predict future weather patterns based on the patterns.
Feedback is given on weather predictions.
… changes future predictions based on this feedback.
It could learn what weather occurs at certain times of year.
… and use this to predict what the weather would be like at the same time of year.
… and adapt its rules/processes for weather predictions.

questions.text: Data is sent from an employee's computer to a server. The data is correct when it is sent, but an error is detected when the data is received. Describe how an error can occur in the data during transmission.

marking_points.text: … that could have caused data loss/gained/changed.

questions.text:
Data is sent from an employee's computer to a server. Methods of error detection and correction can be added to make sure that errors are identified after data is transmitted. Complete the statements about methods of error detection and correction. Use terms from the list. Not all terms need to be used. You should only use each term once.
acknowledgement  automatic repeat query (ARQ)  binary  bit
byte  calculation  check digit  checksum  correct
echo check  error  even  incorrect  negative
number  odd  positive  timeout

A parity check is set to be [blank] or [blank]. A parity [blank] is added to each byte before transmission to make it match the set parity.

An [blank] involves comparing the data sent to the data received back from the receiving device.

An [blank] uses acknowledgement and [blank]. The acknowledgement system can be [blank] or [blank].

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term in the correct place.
